code: @Manual{,   title = {flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models},   author = {Carl Pearson and Weston Voglesonger},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://accidda.github.io/flexstanr/}, }

what: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: portable Stan-backend layer R packages fit Stan model. gives host package one interface fitting either rstan (optionally) cmdstanr, code works whichever backend installed. flexstanr compiles Stan : host package supplies compiled models, flexstanr resolves calling package run time.

title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: stan_options() collects validates sampler arguments chosen backend, forwarding verbatim calls feel native. Mixing one backend’s argument vocabulary errors “mean” hint. fit_model() dispatches fit backend recorded options, resolving compiled model name calling package. fit-consumption accessors read fit backend-agnostically: backend_draws_array(), backend_extract(), backend_generate_quantities(), backend_has_draws().

title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: flexstanr yet CRAN. Install development version GitHub: default backend, rstan, hard dependency. cmdstanr optional backend; CRAN, project wants installs separately (see cmdstanr getting-started guide).

title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: Declare flexstanr dependency call fitting code: model name resolved package’s compiled models (stanmodels rstan, inst/stan/<name>.stan cmdstanr); calling package detected automatically. useFlexStanR() setup helper wire dependencies host package planned. Note. flexstanr began use_standalone() script vendored ACCIDDA Stan packages (imuGAP, hestia, SeverityEstimate). now proper package consumers import rather copy. See flexstanr 0.1.0 CRAN release milestone.
code: # in your package opts <- flexstanr::stan_options(chains = 4, iter = 2000, seed = 1) fit  <- flexstanr::fit_model(   "coverage",              # resolved from your package's stanmodels / inst/stan   dat_stan  = data_list,   init      = init_list,   stan_opts = opts ) draws <- flexstanr::backend_draws_array(fit)

what: Assert a backend name is valid and its package is installed — assert_backend_available
title: Assert a backend name is valid and its package is installed — assert_backend_available
text: Validates backend known choices (also subsumes match.arg()) , optional cmdstanr backend, package installed. rstan always available (hard dependency); cmdstanr optional, selecting without package installed fails early rather deep inside fit. Returns validated backend invisibly.

what: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
text: Detect degenerate "draws" case fit, caller can fail loudly instead returning empty fit. backend-aware: rstan returns mode-2 stanfit empty @sim sampler fails initialize (rather erroring), cmdstanr exposes draws $draws(). Unrecognized objects (e.g. test mocks) treated draws pass untouched.

title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
text: raw_fit backend-native fit object (rstan stanfit cmdstanr CmdStanMCMC).

title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
text: logical; TRUE fit carries usable draws.

title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
code: # Unrecognized objects are treated as carrying draws (pass-through). backend_has_draws(list()) #> [1] TRUE

what: Fit a Stan model through the chosen backend — fit_model
title: Fit a Stan model through the chosen backend — fit_model
text: Dispatches fit backend recorded stan_opts (stan_options()). compiled model resolved model_name calling package: "rstan", package::stanmodels[[model_name]]; "cmdstanr", inst/stan/<model_name>.stan package. calling package detected automatically can overridden package.

title: Fit a Stan model through the chosen backend — fit_model
code: fit_model(   model_name,   dat_stan,   init,   stan_opts,   drop_pars = NULL,   package = caller_package() )

title: Fit a Stan model through the chosen backend — fit_model
text: model_name name Stan model; used look compiled model calling package's stanmodels (rstan) locate .stan source file inst/stan/ (cmdstanr). dat_stan Stan data list. init init list, sized chain count. stan_opts validated stan_options() list (carrying backend element). drop_pars character vector parameter names exclude saved draws, NULL keep everything. Honored rstan; cmdstanr drop parameters warns requested. package name host package whose model fit. Defaults package called fit_model(), correct usual case host package fitting one models.

title: Fit a Stan model through the chosen backend — fit_model
text: backend's fit object (stanfit CmdStanMCMC).

title: Fit a Stan model through the chosen backend — fit_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # From inside a host package that ships a compiled `coverage` model: opts <- stan_options(chains = 2, iter = 500, seed = 1) fit <- fit_model("coverage", dat_stan = data_list, init = init_list,                  stan_opts = opts) } # }

what: flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models — flexstanr-package
title: flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models — flexstanr-package
text: Gives 'Stan'-based R package one interface fitting models either 'rstan' (optionally) 'cmdstanr'. Collects validates sampler options, guarding mixing one backend's argument vocabulary , dispatches fit chosen backend, exposes backend-agnostic accessors reading posterior draws, extracting parameters, running generated quantities. host package supplies compiled models; flexstanr resolves calling package run time, code works whichever backend installed.

title: flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models — flexstanr-package
text: Maintainer: Carl Pearson carl.ab.pearson@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Carl Pearson carl.ab.pearson@gmail.com (ORCID) Weston Voglesonger westonvogle@gmail.com

title: Resolve a host package's compiled rstan model — get_stanmodel

Resolve a host package's compiled rstan model — get_stanmodel

title: Resolve a host package's compiled rstan model — get_stanmodel

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
text: Collects validates sampler arguments chosen backend, forwarding verbatim calls feel native backend. Use backend's argument names; mixing one backend's vocabulary errors hint. model object supplied separately (via fit_model()), data init constructed internally, none may set . chains defaults 4 downstream code can always size per-chain structures .

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
text: ... sampler arguments forwarded verbatim chosen backend's sampler. Use backend's names: "rstan", rstan::sampling() arguments (iter, cores, seed); "cmdstanr", $sample() arguments (iter_warmup, iter_sampling, parallel_chains, ...). chains positive integer specifying number Markov chains.     default 4. backend Stan interface target, one "rstan" (default) "cmdstanr". Determines argument vocabulary accepted sampler fit_model() calls. Selecting "cmdstanr" errors cmdstanr package installed.

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
text: named list validated sampler arguments, carrying backend element recording backend built

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
code: stan_options() #> $chains #> [1] 4 #>  #> $backend #> [1] "rstan" #>  stan_options(chains = 2, iter = 500) #> $iter #> [1] 500 #>  #> $chains #> [1] 2 #>  #> $backend #> [1] "rstan" #>  if (requireNamespace("cmdstanr", quietly = TRUE)) {   stan_options(backend = "cmdstanr", parallel_chains = 4, iter_warmup = 500) } #> $parallel_chains #> [1] 4 #>  #> $iter_warmup #> [1] 500 #>  #> $chains #> [1] 4 #>  #> $backend #> [1] "cmdstanr" #>
